name: Microsoft Binary Suspicious Communication Endpoint
notes:
This rule detects an executable in the Windows folder accessing suspicious domains

Covered from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
SIGMA Title: Microsoft Binary Suspicious Communication Endpoint
SIGMA Description: Detects an executable in the Windows folder accessing suspicious domains


Confidence: HIGH

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following Access.ACL Permit
when the event matches Initiated (custom) is any of true

when the event matches Destination Hostname (custom) contains any of [dl.dropboxusercontent.com or pastebin.com or cdn.discordapp.com/attachments or mediafire.com or mega.nz or ddns.net or paste.ee or hastebin.com/raw or ghostbin.co or ufile.io or anonfiles.com or send.exploit.in or transfer.sh or privatlab.net or privatlab.com or sendspace.com or pastetext.net or pastebin.pl or api.dropboxapi or script.google.com or script.googleusercontent.com]
when the event matches Process Path (custom) contains any of [C:\Windows or C:\Users\Public]
TD_list: APPLY Microsoft Binary Suspicious Communication Endpoint on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following Access.ACL Permit
AND when the event matches Initiated (custom) is any of true

AND when the event matches Destination Hostname (custom) contains any of [dl.dropboxusercontent.com or pastebin.com or cdn.discordapp.com/attachments or mediafire.com or mega.nz or ddns.net or paste.ee or hastebin.com/raw or ghostbin.co or ufile.io or anonfiles.com or send.exploit.in or transfer.sh or privatlab.net or privatlab.com or sendspace.com or pastetext.net or pastebin.pl or api.dropboxapi or script.google.com or script.googleusercontent.com]
AND when the event matches Process Path (custom) contains any of [C:\Windows or C:\Users\Public]
RED_list: Event name: Microsoft Binary Suspicious Communication Endpoint
Event description: Detects an executable in the Windows folder accessing suspicious domains
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
